name: Parslied Buttermilk Biscuits
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 500°F.
2. Lightly grease a large cookie sheet.
3. Sift together 1 1/2 cups of flour and salt in a large bowl; set remaining flour aside in a small bowl.
4. Cut shortening into the flour mixture with a pastry blender or fork until it resembles coarse meal. Stir in chopped parsley.
5. Pour buttermilk all at once and stir with a fork until the dough just holds together. Do not overwork; the dough should be wet and slightly lumpy.
6. Flour hands with reserved flour, then shape 1/4 cup of dough into a ball between your palms. Toss lightly in the bowl to coat, then roll gently into a smooth ball.
7. Place biscuit on prepared cookie sheet. Repeat with remaining dough, using extra flour as needed and placing biscuits so they touch slightly.
8. Gently pat each biscuit to flatten slightly.
9. Bake for 8-10 minutes until golden brown. Makes about 8 biscuits.
